comments.comment_text: Please state if this was a calibration or a verification. (Were the initial values all zeroed, or previous values applied when running lines)
comments.response_text:
It has been updated.
“This two-step procedure was performed in accordance with standard USBL calibration practices”. This is not standard practice.
The tracks will need to be provided as well. My understanding is that it was not a spin test, but the vessel ran a circle around the beacon, which is not a valid calibration method. In any case a spin test is performed after a calibration for verification purposes. Box in, clover leaf or figure of eight are the only line patterns valid for calibration purposes.
This needs to be better explained in order for ALARP certificates to be issued, as a the full presentation of USBL presentation is required as part of acceptance by third party certificate issuers.
If it was a calibration and not a verification, then positional spread first with zero, and then with the calibration should be supplied, which should look very different. Both values spreads look the same. 
Also noted is that priors are used in the calibration and should not. “Use Priors = No”.
This is not to refute the results, but we cannot clearly present this in it’s current form to a third party.
(Please present the spread when the zero values are applied, as it looks like you have presented the two post “calibration”  figures).
The comments regarding the USBL calibration procedure have been reviewed internally. At the project site, the initial procedure was carried out based on prior experience with similar conditions in past projects. However, in response to the request from the Client Representative (CR), the final calibration was performed using a valid line pattern, specifically the Box-in method.
Accordingly, after receiving the comment, the software was re-run with the “Use Priors = No” setting, and it was confirmed that there was no significant difference in the results. The potential impact of this setting on the final calibration outcome will be further clarified once we receive an official response from the manufacturer. The CASIUS calibration report generated with the “Use Priors = No” setting has been included in Appendix A.
Appreciated the update especially the addition of the, but to be sure on the point of tracks the beacon is assumed to be in the centre of the GNSS positions. If this is the case then the initial circular run is NOT a valid calibration.

Only if they are offset, which is not evident from the report. No problem with the box-in. “ Initially, the calibration was conducted based on prior operational experience under similar conditions.” Does not explain the process.
Thank you for your detailed comments. As you commented, we understand that the circular run is not a valid calibration method for this project; therefore, all related references have been removed from the report. The final calibration was carried out using the valid ‘box-in’ line pattern. We appreciate your valuable feedback, which has also been documented internally for reference in future projects.
comments.summary_ko: 교정(calibration)인지 검증(verification)인지 명시. 초기값 영점 적용 또는 이전값 적용 여부.

comments.section:
7.1.3 System Verification
Table 27. Average Ping Density, p.24
comments.comment_text:
Scale on Table only show up  9 ping hits, not 10 as mentioned in below comment. Please use a more appropriate scale.
'The table presents the average ping density for the 1.0 m cell size, confirming that the client’s requirement for a minimum of 10 pings per cell was successfully met'.
comments.response_text:
It has been updated.
Still exactly the same scale.
ALSO, the scale needs to reflect better the 
Please change the scale to better display the range of hits per bin (30 or 40 perhaps?) 
The water depth in the survey area ranges from approximately 50 to 65 meters. For this project, a sounding density of 9 hits or more within this depth range is considered sufficient to meet the requirement. Therefore, the maximum scale was set to 9 in order to verify whether the sounding density meets the specified criteria.
Table 28 is intended only to present the average ping density within the survey area.
The mobilisation Report should display the system health etc, not just if the spec is reached or not. Please change the scale to better display the range of hits per bin as requested.
It has been updated.
comments.summary_ko: Table 스케일에 9 ping만 표시(10 아님). 적절한 스케일 사용. 1.0m 셀 평균 ping 밀도 확인.

comments.comment_text: Low Frequency and High Frequency are all relative so please replace all references to LF and HF with the actual frequencies used.
comments.response_text:
In Section 8, the main text specifies the frequencies for both low-frequency and high-frequency. For readability, the terms LF and HF were used thereafter.
Would you like us to include the corresponding frequencies every time LF and HF are mentioned?
Yes please. The primary SSS has three frequencies. And the spare fish has different frequencies to the primary fish. Hence the request to state the actual frequencies used. (This is a key factor for 3rd Party ALARP validation).
It has been updated.
comments.summary_ko: LF/HF를 실제 사용 주파수로 교체. 상대적 표현 지양.

comments.comment_text: Please add the range testing for the different frequency fish.
comments.response_text:
Both the high-frequency and low-frequency channels were acquired with a range setting of 100 m per side, and this information has been included in Section 8.1.3 and 8.1.4 (Figure).
The 850 kHz was also tested. Please present these images as well.

It has been updated in Chapter 8.1.5
comments.summary_ko: 주파수별 피쉬 레인지 테스트 결과 추가.
